---
title: "Maladie à Virus Ebola — Situation Report Extraction"
subtitle: "Democratic Republic of Congo"
author: "Billy J Quilty (Charité Berlin, LSHTM & MSF Epicentre)"
date: today
date-format: "DD MMMM YYYY"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-title: "Contents"
    theme: flatly
    embed-resources: true
    fig-align: center
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
if (!requireNamespace("pacman", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, lubridate, here, scales, ggrepel, flextable, ggh4x, patchwork)

set_flextable_defaults(
  font.family = "Arial",
  font.size   = 10,
  border.color = "#CCCCCC"
)
```

```{r}
#| label: load-data
df_raw <- read_csv(
  here::here("outputs", "master_combined_counts.csv"),
  col_types       = cols(.default = "c"),
  show_col_types  = FALSE
)

df <- df_raw |>
  mutate(
    date        = dmy(count_end_date),
    suspect     = as.numeric(cases_suspect),
    probable    = as.numeric(cases_probable),
    confirmed   = as.numeric(cases_confirmed),
    deaths      = coalesce(as.numeric(deaths_suspected), as.numeric(deaths_probable), as.numeric(deaths_confirmed)),
    deaths_susp = as.numeric(deaths_suspected),
    is_agg      = (toupper(is_aggregate) == "TRUE"),
    # Only sum to zero when at least one component is present; otherwise NA
    # (avoids a false zero for zones that simply weren't reported in that sitrep)
    unconfirmed = if_else(
      !is.na(suspect) | !is.na(probable),
      coalesce(suspect, 0L) + coalesce(probable, 0L),
      NA_real_
    )
  )

# Backfill province for rows where the PDF did not repeat it on every data row.
# Aggregate/total rows are left as-is (their province is intentionally blank or
# a province name used to identify the aggregate level).
ZONE_PROVINCE_LUT <- tribble(
  ~zone,                     ~province_lut,
  "Aru",                     "Ituri",
  "Bambu",                   "Ituri",
  "Bunia",                   "Ituri",
  "Echantillons sans fiche", "Ituri",
  "Kilo",                    "Ituri",
  "Kilo Mission",            "Ituri",
  "Mambasa",                 "Ituri",
  "Mongbwalu",               "Ituri",
  "Nizi",                    "Ituri",
  "Nyankunde",               "Ituri",
  "Rwampara",                "Ituri",
  "Butembo",                 "Nord-Kivu",
  "Goma",                    "Nord-Kivu",
  "Kalunguta",               "Nord-Kivu",
  "Karisimbi",               "Nord-Kivu",
  "Karissibi",               "Nord-Kivu",
  "Katwa",                   "Nord-Kivu",
  "Kyondo",                  "Nord-Kivu",
  "Oicha",                   "Nord-Kivu",
  "Miti Murhesa",            "Sud-Kivu",
  "Miti-Murhesa",            "Sud-Kivu",
)

df <- df |>
  left_join(ZONE_PROVINCE_LUT, by = "zone") |>
  mutate(province = if_else(!is_agg & is.na(province), province_lut, province)) |>
  select(-province_lut)

ZONE_LEVELS <- c(
  "Mongbwalu", "Rwampara", "Bunia", "Nyankunde", "Bambu",
  "Butembo", "Katwa", "Goma"
)

# Colorblind-safe palette — Wong (2011)
PAL <- c(
  "Suspect/probable cases" = "#56B4E9",
  "Confirmed cases"        = "#D55E00",
  "Outbreak deaths"        = "#404040"
)

int_breaks <- function(x) unique(floor(pretty(x)))

theme_sitrep <- function(base_size = 11) {
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) %+replace%
    theme(
      plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size + 2, hjust = 0),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = base_size - 1, colour = "grey45", hjust = 0,
                                      margin = margin(b = 8)),
      plot.caption     = element_text(size = base_size - 3, colour = "grey55", hjust = 1,
                                      margin = margin(t = 8)),
      plot.margin      = margin(12, 16, 8, 12),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey92"),
      legend.position  = "bottom",
      legend.key.size  = unit(0.75, "lines"),
      legend.text      = element_text(size = base_size - 1),
      strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = base_size - 1,
                                      margin = margin(b = 4)),
      axis.title       = element_text(size = base_size - 1, colour = "grey35"),
      axis.text        = element_text(size = base_size - 2, colour = "grey25")
    )
}

# ── Derived subsets ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
totals <- df |>
  filter(count_type == "Cumules", is_agg, zone == "Total") |>
  group_by(date) |>
  slice_max(order_by = unconfirmed, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  arrange(date) |>
  mutate(deaths = cummax(replace_na(deaths, 0L)))

zone_ts <- df |>
  filter(count_type == "Cumules", !is_agg, zone %in% ZONE_LEVELS) |>
  group_by(date, zone, province) |>
  slice_max(order_by = unconfirmed, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(zone = factor(zone, levels = ZONE_LEVELS)) |>
  arrange(zone, date) |>
  group_by(zone) |>
  mutate(
    # Flag raw downward revisions before enforcing monotonicity
    confirmed_raw   = confirmed,
    unconfirmed_raw = unconfirmed,
    deaths_raw      = deaths,
    deaths_susp_raw = deaths_susp,
    rev_confirmed   = confirmed   < lag(cummax(replace_na(confirmed,   0L)), default = 0L),
    rev_unconfirmed = unconfirmed < lag(cummax(replace_na(unconfirmed, 0L)), default = 0L),
    # Apply cummax only over observed values; leave NAs before the first
    # real observation so geom_line breaks rather than drawing from zero
    unconfirmed = {x <- unconfirmed; i <- !is.na(x); if (any(i)) x[i] <- cummax(x[i]); x},
    confirmed   = {x <- confirmed;   i <- !is.na(x); if (any(i)) x[i] <- cummax(x[i]); x},
    deaths      = {x <- deaths;      i <- !is.na(x); if (any(i)) x[i] <- cummax(x[i]); x},
    deaths_susp = {x <- deaths_susp; i <- !is.na(x); if (any(i)) x[i] <- cummax(x[i]); x}
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# Warn about any downward revisions that cummax suppressed
revisions <- zone_ts |>
  filter(rev_confirmed | rev_unconfirmed) |>
  transmute(
    zone, date,
    confirmed_raw, confirmed,
    unconfirmed_raw, unconfirmed
  )
if (nrow(revisions) > 0) {
  message("NOTE: cummax suppressed ", nrow(revisions),
          " downward revision(s) in cumulative counts:\n",
          paste(capture.output(print(revisions)), collapse = "\n"))
}

zone_new <- df |>
  filter(count_type == "Nouveaux", !is_agg, zone %in% ZONE_LEVELS) |>
  group_by(date, zone, province) |>
  slice_max(order_by = unconfirmed, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(zone = factor(zone, levels = ZONE_LEVELS)) |>
  arrange(zone, date)

daily_new <- df |>
  filter(count_type == "Nouveaux", is_agg, zone == "Total") |>
  group_by(date) |>
  slice_max(order_by = unconfirmed, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  arrange(date)

make_zone_labels <- function(dat) {
  dat |>
    mutate(
      zone_label = paste0(zone, " (", province, ")"),
      zone_label = factor(zone_label,
        levels = dat |>
          distinct(zone, province) |>
          arrange(zone) |>
          mutate(lbl = paste0(zone, " (", province, ")")) |>
          pull(lbl)
      )
    )
}

# ── Inline summary statistics ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
latest_total <- totals |> slice_max(date, n = 1)
latest_date  <- format(max(totals$date, na.rm = TRUE), "%d %B %Y")
n_unconf     <- latest_total$unconfirmed
n_conf       <- latest_total$confirmed
n_deaths     <- coalesce(latest_total$deaths, 0L)
n_total      <- n_unconf + n_conf
cfr_pct      <- if (n_total > 0) round(n_deaths / n_total * 100, 1) else NA_real_
n_zones      <- n_distinct(zone_ts$zone)
n_provinces  <- n_distinct(zone_ts$province)
```

```{r}
#| label: load-kraemer
# INRB-UMIE canonical name → this extraction zone name
KRAEMER_NAME_MAP <- c("Mongbalu" = "Mongbwalu", "Nyakunde" = "Nyankunde")

read_kraemer_long <- function(filename) {
  read_csv(
    here::here(
      "Ebola_DRC_2026", "data", "insp_sitrep", "processed",
      paste0(filename, "__daily.csv")
    ),
    col_types      = cols(.default = "c"),
    show_col_types = FALSE
  )
}

parse_kraemer_date <- function(x) {
  coalesce(dmy(x, quiet = TRUE), ymd(x, quiet = TRUE), mdy(x, quiet = TRUE))
}

kraemer <- read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__cumulative_confirmed_cases") |>
  rename(confirmed = cumulative_confirmed_cases) |>
  full_join(
    read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__cumulative_suspected_cases") |>
      rename(suspected = cumulative_suspected_cases),
    by = c("nom", "date")
  ) |>
  full_join(
    read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__cumulative_suspected_deaths") |>
      rename(deaths_susp = cumulative_suspected_deaths),
    by = c("nom", "date")
  ) |>
  full_join(
    read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__cumulative_confirmed_deaths") |>
      rename(deaths_conf = cumulative_confirmed_deaths),
    by = c("nom", "date")
  ) |>
  mutate(
    zone       = recode(nom, !!!KRAEMER_NAME_MAP),
    date       = parse_kraemer_date(date),
    confirmed  = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(confirmed)),
    suspected  = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(suspected)),
    deaths_susp = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(deaths_susp)),
    deaths_conf = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(deaths_conf)),
    # Total deaths = suspected + confirmed (mirrors our coalesce logic)
    deaths     = coalesce(deaths_susp, 0) + coalesce(deaths_conf, 0),
    deaths     = if_else(is.na(deaths_susp) & is.na(deaths_conf), NA_real_, deaths),
    source     = "INRB-UMIE"
  ) |>
  select(zone, date, confirmed, suspected, deaths_susp, deaths_conf, deaths, source) |>
  filter(zone %in% ZONE_LEVELS)

kraemer_hosp <- read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__hospitalised") |>
  mutate(
    zone         = recode(nom, !!!KRAEMER_NAME_MAP),
    date         = parse_kraemer_date(date),
    hospitalised = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(hospitalised))
  )

kraemer_contacts <- read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__cumulative_contacts_traced") |>
  mutate(
    zone            = recode(nom, !!!KRAEMER_NAME_MAP),
    date            = parse_kraemer_date(date),
    contacts_traced = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(cumulative_contacts_traced))
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(contacts_traced))

# Response indicators (hospitalisations by zone)
kraemer_response <- read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__hospitalised") |>
  full_join(read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__in_bed_previous_day"), by = c("nom", "date")) |>
  full_join(
    read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__new_hosp_admissions") |>
      rename(total_admissions = new_all_admissions),
    by = c("nom", "date")
  ) |>
  full_join(
    read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__new_hosp_detainees") |>
      rename(new_admissions_ppl = new_hosp_detainees),
    by = c("nom", "date")
  ) |>
  full_join(
    read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__new_hosp_other") |>
      rename(new_admissions_other = new_other),
    by = c("nom", "date")
  ) |>
  mutate(
    zone   = recode(nom, !!!KRAEMER_NAME_MAP),
    date   = parse_kraemer_date(date),
    across(c(hospitalised, in_bed_previous_day, total_admissions,
             new_admissions_ppl, new_admissions_other),
           ~suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.x))),
    source = "INRB-UMIE"
  ) |>
  filter(zone %in% ZONE_LEVELS)

# POE (checkpoint-level in INRB-UMIE; summed to daily aggregate for comparison)
kraemer_poe <- read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__total_poe_passed") |>
  rename(total_passed = total_poe_passed) |>
  full_join(
    read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__total_poe_screened") |>
      rename(total_screened = total_poe_screened),
    by = c("nom", "date")
  ) |>
  full_join(
    read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__total_poe_hand_washing") |>
      rename(total_handwashing = total_poe_hand_washing),
    by = c("nom", "date")
  ) |>
  full_join(
    read_kraemer_long("insp_sitrep__total_poe_sanitised") |>
      rename(total_sensitised = total_poe_sanitised),
    by = c("nom", "date")
  ) |>
  mutate(
    date = parse_kraemer_date(date),
    across(c(total_passed, total_screened, total_handwashing, total_sensitised),
           ~suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.x)))
  ) |>
  group_by(date) |>
  summarise(
    across(c(total_passed, total_screened, total_handwashing, total_sensitised),
           ~sum(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(source = "INRB-UMIE")
```

```{r}
#| label: load-bvd-reference
# BVDOutbreakSize PDF-scanned national reference (SitReps 009-012, 23-26 May)
# Source: github.com/epiforecasts/BVDOutbreakSize PR #140
bvd_ref <- read_csv(
  here::here("outputs", "bvd_outbreak_size_national_reference.csv"),
  col_types      = cols(.default = "c"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) |>
  mutate(
    date          = ymd(report_date),
    bvd_sitrep    = paste0("SitRep ", sitrep),
    bvd_suspected = as.numeric(suspected_cases),
    bvd_confirmed = as.numeric(confirmed_cases),
    bvd_deaths    = as.numeric(suspected_deaths)
  ) |>
  transmute(date, bvd_sitrep, bvd_suspected, bvd_confirmed, bvd_deaths, bvd_notes = notes)

# INRB-UMIE national totals (single snapshot: 27 May 2026)
read_kraemer_national_snapshot <- function(filename, value_col) {
  read_kraemer_long(filename) |>
    rename(value = all_of(value_col)) |>
    mutate(
      date  = parse_kraemer_date(date),
      value = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(value))
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(date)) |>
    group_by(date) |>
    summarise(value = first(value), .groups = "drop")
}

parsed_kraemer_nat <-
  read_kraemer_national_snapshot(
    "insp_sitrep__national_cumulative_suspected_cases",
    "national_cumulative_suspected_cases"
  ) |> rename(kraemer_suspected = value) |>
  full_join(
    read_kraemer_national_snapshot(
      "insp_sitrep__national_cumulative_confirmed_cases",
      "national_cumulative_confirmed_cases"
    ) |> rename(kraemer_confirmed = value),
    by = "date"
  ) |>
  full_join(
    read_kraemer_national_snapshot(
      "insp_sitrep__national_cumulative_suspected_deaths",
      "national_cumulative_suspected_deaths"
    ) |> rename(kraemer_susp_deaths = value),
    by = "date"
  ) |>
  full_join(
    read_kraemer_national_snapshot(
      "insp_sitrep__national_cumulative_confirmed_deaths",
      "national_cumulative_confirmed_deaths"
    ) |> rename(kraemer_conf_deaths = value),
    by = "date"
  )

# Pull our national aggregate Cumules rows
# Priority: province=="Total" > zone=="Total" > both NA
ous_national <- df_raw |>
  filter(
    count_type == "Cumules",
    toupper(is_aggregate) == "TRUE",
    (zone == "Total" | province == "Total" | (is.na(zone) & is.na(province)))
  ) |>
  mutate(
    date      = dmy(count_end_date),
    suspected = as.numeric(cases_suspect),
    confirmed = as.numeric(cases_confirmed),
    deaths    = coalesce(
      as.numeric(deaths_suspected),
      as.numeric(deaths_probable),
      as.numeric(deaths_confirmed)
    ),
    row_priority = case_when(
      province == "Total" ~ 3L,
      zone == "Total"     ~ 2L,
      TRUE                ~ 1L
    )
  ) |>
  group_by(date) |>
  slice_max(order_by = row_priority, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  transmute(date, sitrep_source, ours_suspected = suspected,
            ours_confirmed = confirmed, ours_deaths = deaths)

# Join all three sources
nat_comparison <- ous_national |>
  full_join(bvd_ref,           by = "date") |>
  full_join(parsed_kraemer_nat, by = "date") |>
  arrange(date)
```

::: {.callout-warning}
**Automated extraction — verification required.**
Case counts and figures in this report are extracted from INSP DRC PDF situation reports by an AI vision model (Anthropic Claude `claude-sonnet-4-6`) without systematic manual verification of every value. Extraction errors are possible, particularly where PDF table layouts are complex or inconsistent. All values should be verified against the [original INSP DRC situation reports](https://insp.cd/ebola/) before any operational, clinical, or policy use. This report is intended for research and situational awareness only.
:::

## Introduction

This report summarises the 2026 Ebola (MVE) outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, covering cases in Ituri and Nord-Kivu provinces. Data are drawn from official situation reports published by the Institut National de Santé Publique (INSP DRC) and are updated automatically as new reports become available.

## Methods

### Data sources

Case counts were taken from the INSP DRC situation report PDFs, downloaded automatically from <https://insp.cd/ebola/>. We verified that our copies of each PDF are identical to those used by INRB-UMIE (by comparing file checksums), so any differences between the two datasets come from how the tables were read, not which documents were used. The INRB-UMIE series, produced by manual data entry, was used as an independent check on our automated extraction.

### Automated PDF extraction

Each PDF was read by Claude (Anthropic, `claude-sonnet-4-6`), which looks at the page visually — the same way a person would — rather than relying on the underlying text. This allows it to handle the complex table layouts in the situation reports accurately. From each PDF we extract four things: daily new case counts, cumulative case and death totals, patient movement through isolation units (available from SitRep 006 onwards), and Point of Entry screening activity. A full text transcript of each report is also saved for audit purposes.

### Data harmonisation

Health zone names were standardised to a consistent spelling across all reports. Suspected and probable case counts were added together into a single "unconfirmed" category, since different reports label these columns differently. Cumulative totals were not allowed to decrease over time (a count cannot go backwards), and where more than one row existed for the same zone and date, the higher value was kept.

### Statistical analysis

All analysis and figures were produced in R (version `r R.version$major`.`r R.version$minor`) using the `tidyverse` [@tidyverse], `ggplot2` [@ggplot2], and `flextable` [@flextable] packages. This report was rendered automatically with Quarto.

## Results

As of `r latest_date`, the DRC MVE outbreak had recorded a cumulative total of `r prettyNum(n_total, big.mark = ",")` cases (`r prettyNum(n_conf, big.mark = ",")` confirmed, `r prettyNum(n_unconf, big.mark = ",")` suspect or probable) and `r prettyNum(n_deaths, big.mark = ",")` deaths, corresponding to a case fatality ratio of `r cfr_pct`%. Cases have been reported across `r n_zones` health zones in `r n_provinces` provinces.

### Cumulative epidemic curve

```{r}
#| label: fig-cumulative
#| fig-cap: "Cumulative MVE cases and deaths, DRC. Suspect and probable cases are combined. End-of-line labels show the latest recorded value. Source: INSP DRC."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
p1_dat <- totals |>
  select(date,
         `Suspect/probable cases` = unconfirmed,
         `Confirmed cases`        = confirmed,
         `Outbreak deaths`        = deaths) |>
  pivot_longer(-date, names_to = "category", values_to = "count") |>
  mutate(category = factor(category, levels = names(PAL)))

p1_last <- p1_dat |>
  filter(!is.na(count)) |>
  group_by(category) |>
  slice_max(date, n = 1) |>
  ungroup()

p <- ggplot(p1_dat, aes(x = date, y = count, colour = category, group = category)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_label_repel(
    data           = p1_last,
    aes(label      = count),
    size           = 3.5,
    nudge_x        = 0.8,
    direction      = "y",
    hjust          = 0,
    segment.colour = "grey70",
    segment.size   = 0.3,
    show.legend    = FALSE,
    na.rm          = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = PAL) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_labels = "%d %b",
    date_breaks = "2 days",
    expand      = expansion(add = c(0, 1.2))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = int_breaks, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.08))) +
  labs(
    title    = "MVE DRC \u2014 Cumulative epidemic curve",
    subtitle = "Total counts across all health zones",
    x = NULL, y = "Cumulative count", colour = NULL,
    caption  = "Source: INSP DRC situation reports"
  ) +
  theme_sitrep(base_size = 12)
ggsave(here("outputs", "plots", "fig-cumulative.png"), plot = p, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
p
```

### Daily new cases

```{r}
#| label: fig-new-cases
#| fig-cap: "New cases reported per situation report across all health zones, DRC. Source: INSP DRC."
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
p <- daily_new |>
  select(date,
         `Suspect/probable cases` = unconfirmed,
         `Confirmed cases`        = confirmed) |>
  pivot_longer(-date, names_to = "category", values_to = "count") |>
  mutate(category = factor(category, levels = c("Suspect/probable cases", "Confirmed cases"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = date, y = count, fill = category)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.3, na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = PAL) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%d %b", date_breaks = "1 day") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = int_breaks, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  labs(
    title    = "MVE DRC \u2014 Daily new cases",
    subtitle = "Total reported per situation report",
    x = NULL, y = "New cases", fill = NULL,
    caption  = "Source: INSP DRC situation reports"
  ) +
  theme_sitrep(base_size = 12)
ggsave(here("outputs", "plots", "fig-new-cases.png"), plot = p, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 300)
p
```

### Geographic distribution — cumulative counts

```{r}
#| label: fig-zone-cumulative
#| fig-cap: "Cumulative cases by health zone. Y-axis is free across panels. Source: INSP DRC."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 11
p <- zone_ts |>
  make_zone_labels() |>
  select(date, zone_label,
         `Suspect/probable cases` = unconfirmed,
         `Confirmed cases`        = confirmed,
         `Outbreak deaths`        = deaths) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(`Suspect/probable cases`, `Confirmed cases`, `Outbreak deaths`),
    names_to  = "category",
    values_to = "count"
  ) |>
  mutate(category = factor(category, levels = names(PAL))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = date, y = count, colour = category, group = category)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = PAL) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%d %b", date_breaks = "1 day") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = int_breaks, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  facet_wrap(~ zone_label, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    title    = "MVE DRC \u2014 Cumulative cases by health zone",
    subtitle = "Each panel shows one health zone; y-axis is free.",
    x = NULL, y = "Cumulative count", colour = NULL,
    caption  = "Source: INSP DRC situation reports"
  ) +
  theme_sitrep(base_size = 10) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggsave(here("outputs", "plots", "fig-zone-cumulative.png"), plot = p, width = 10, height = 11, dpi = 300)
p
```

### Geographic distribution — new cases

```{r}
#| label: fig-zone-new
#| fig-cap: "New cases per situation report by health zone. Y-axis is free across panels. Source: INSP DRC."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 11
p <- zone_new |>
  make_zone_labels() |>
  select(date, zone_label,
         `Suspect/probable cases` = unconfirmed,
         `Confirmed cases`        = confirmed) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(`Suspect/probable cases`, `Confirmed cases`),
    names_to  = "category",
    values_to = "count"
  ) |>
  mutate(category = factor(category, levels = c("Suspect/probable cases", "Confirmed cases"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = date, y = count, fill = category)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.4, na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = PAL) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%d %b", date_breaks = "1 day") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = int_breaks, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  facet_wrap(~ zone_label, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    title    = "MVE DRC \u2014 Daily new cases by health zone",
    subtitle = "Per situation report; y-axis is free.",
    x = NULL, y = "New cases", fill = NULL,
    caption  = "Source: INSP DRC situation reports"
  ) +
  theme_sitrep(base_size = 10) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, angle = 45, hjust = 1))
ggsave(here("outputs", "plots", "fig-zone-new.png"), plot = p, width = 10, height = 11, dpi = 300)
p
```

### Current counts by health zone

Table 1 presents the most recent cumulative case counts disaggregated by health zone, drawn from the latest available situation report for each zone.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-zones
#| tbl-cap: "Table 1. Latest cumulative case counts by health zone."
zone_latest <- zone_ts |>
  group_by(zone, province) |>
  slice_max(date, n = 1) |>
  ungroup() |>
  arrange(zone) |>
  transmute(
    `Health zone`      = as.character(zone),
    Province           = province,
    `Latest sitrep`    = format(date, "%d %b %Y"),
    `Suspect/probable` = unconfirmed,
    `Confirmed`        = confirmed,
    `Deaths`           = deaths
  )

total_row <- tibble(
  `Health zone`      = "Total",
  Province           = "\u2014",
  `Latest sitrep`    = format(max(totals$date, na.rm = TRUE), "%d %b %Y"),
  `Suspect/probable` = sum(zone_latest$`Suspect/probable`, na.rm = TRUE),
  `Confirmed`        = sum(zone_latest$`Confirmed`,        na.rm = TRUE),
  `Deaths`           = sum(zone_latest$`Deaths`,           na.rm = TRUE)
)

bind_rows(zone_latest, total_row) |>
  flextable() |>
  bold(part = "header") |>
  bold(i = nrow(zone_latest) + 1L) |>
  bg(part = "header", bg = "#1A3A5C") |>
  color(part = "header", color = "white") |>
  bg(i = nrow(zone_latest) + 1L, bg = "#E8EFF7") |>
  hline(i = nrow(zone_latest), part = "body") |>
  align(j = 4:6, align = "right", part = "all") |>
  colformat_int(j = 4:6) |>
  autofit()
```

### Cross-source validation

We checked our automated extraction against the [INRB-UMIE Ebola DRC 2026](https://github.com/INRB-UMIE/Ebola_DRC_2026) manually coded dataset. For SitReps 1–11, both datasets work from the same PDF files, so any differences between them reflect how the tables were read rather than which documents were used. From SitRep 12 onwards, INRB-UMIE received health-zone tables directly from INRB via WhatsApp; these may not correspond exactly to the figures published in each PDF. Two zone name variants between the datasets (Mongbwalu/Mongbalu and Nyankunde/Nyakunde) were matched manually. Values marked "ND" (not available) were treated as missing.

#### National-level comparison — this repo, BVDOutbreakSize, and INRB-UMIE

At the national level, cumulative suspected and confirmed case counts agree exactly across all three sources for every date in the overlap period. The only divergences are in the deaths series.

From SitRep 10 onwards, INSP's front-page banner figures and the per-health-zone sub-tables frequently report different national totals. INRB-UMIE tracks both separately: files named `national_*` contain the banner figures, which represent INSP's official national position; files without `national` in the name contain the zone-level sub-table values. This repo also reads from the front-page banner. BVDOutbreakSize, by contrast, derives national totals by summing the per-zone rows. The disagreement between these approaches therefore reflects a structural feature of the source documents — INSP's own banner and sub-tables often disagree — rather than an error in either dataset.

On 23 May (SitRep 009), this repo and the INRB-UMIE national figure both record 119 suspected deaths, matching the front-page banner. [BVDOutbreakSize](https://github.com/epiforecasts/BVDOutbreakSize/pull/140) (PR \#140) records 220, which is the per-zone row sum. Both values are internally consistent with their respective source tables. On 26 May (SitRep 012), both a first and a revised version of the PDF were processed. The original extraction (SitRep\_012) records 238 deaths (matching BVDOutbreakSize), while the revised extraction (SitRep\_012\_v2) records 246 deaths; there was no per-zone case table in this report against which to cross-check the banner figure. The INRB-UMIE national snapshot currently covers 27 May only (back-filling to SitRep 10 is in progress); on that date their banner-derived figures are 906 suspected cases, 223 suspected deaths, and 17 confirmed deaths, consistent with this repo.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-national-comparison
#| tbl-cap: "National cumulative totals — cross-source comparison. Shading indicates values that differ across sources. BVD: BVDOutbreakSize (epiforecasts/BVDOutbreakSize PR \\#140); INRB-UMIE: INRB-UMIE/Ebola\_DRC\_2026. A dash indicates no data available from that source for that date."
nat_comparison |>
  transmute(
    Date                     = format(date, "%d %b"),
    `Suspect. (ours)`        = ours_suspected,
    `Suspect. (BVD)`         = bvd_suspected,
    `Suspect. (INRB-UMIE)`     = kraemer_suspected,
    `Confirmed (ours)`           = ours_confirmed,
    `Confirmed (BVD)`            = bvd_confirmed,
    `Confirmed (INRB-UMIE)`      = kraemer_confirmed,
    `Deaths (ours)`              = ours_deaths,
    `Deaths (BVD)`               = bvd_deaths,
    `Deaths susp. (INRB-UMIE)`  = kraemer_susp_deaths,
    `Deaths conf. (INRB-UMIE)`  = kraemer_conf_deaths
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  set_header_labels(Date = "Date") |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "Suspected cases", "Confirmed cases", "Deaths"),
    colwidths = c(1, 3, 3, 4)
  ) |>
  merge_h(part = "header") |>
  align(align = "center", part = "header") |>
  align(j = -1, align = "right", part = "body") |>
  # Highlight cells where sources disagree in the Deaths columns
  bg(
    i = ~ !is.na(`Deaths (ours)`) & !is.na(`Deaths (BVD)`) & `Deaths (ours)` != `Deaths (BVD)`,
    j = c("Deaths (ours)", "Deaths (BVD)"),
    bg = "#FFF3CD"
  ) |>
  bg(
    i = ~ !is.na(`Deaths (ours)`) & !is.na(`Deaths susp. (INRB-UMIE)`) &
         `Deaths (ours)` != `Deaths susp. (INRB-UMIE)`,
    j = c("Deaths (ours)", "Deaths susp. (INRB-UMIE)"),
    bg = "#FFF3CD"
  ) |>
  colformat_num(na_str = "—") |>
  colformat_int(j = 2:11, na_str = "—") |>
  bold(part = "header") |>
  hline(part = "body") |>
  autofit()
```

#### Internal consistency — zone sum vs national aggregate

As an internal check, we summed the cumulative counts across all health zones present in each situation report and compared the result with the national aggregate extracted from the front-page banner. Where the two disagree, this typically reflects one of three structural features: early reports that provided only a province-level Ituri total rather than individual zone rows; reports where not all zones were individually listed; or the banner vs sub-table discrepancy that INSP introduced from SitRep 10 onwards (described above). Rows with a non-zero difference are highlighted.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-zone-sum-check
#| tbl-cap: "Zone sum vs national aggregate. Values show the cumulative total across all extracted health zones versus the national figure from the front-page banner. Δ = zone sum minus national banner. Highlighted rows have a non-zero difference in at least one metric."
zone_sum_vs_nat <- zone_ts |>
  group_by(date) |>
  summarise(
    sum_susp = if (any(!is.na(unconfirmed_raw))) sum(unconfirmed_raw, na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
    sum_conf = if (any(!is.na(confirmed_raw)))   sum(confirmed_raw,   na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
    sum_dths = if (any(!is.na(deaths_raw)))       sum(deaths_raw,      na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(
    ous_national |> select(date,
                            nat_susp = ours_suspected,
                            nat_conf = ours_confirmed,
                            nat_dths = ours_deaths),
    by = "date"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    d_susp = sum_susp - nat_susp,
    d_conf = sum_conf - nat_conf,
    d_dths = sum_dths - nat_dths
  ) |>
  arrange(date)

discrepant_rows <- which(
  (!is.na(zone_sum_vs_nat$d_susp) & zone_sum_vs_nat$d_susp != 0) |
  (!is.na(zone_sum_vs_nat$d_conf) & zone_sum_vs_nat$d_conf != 0) |
  (!is.na(zone_sum_vs_nat$d_dths) & zone_sum_vs_nat$d_dths != 0)
)

zone_sum_vs_nat |>
  transmute(
    Date             = format(date, "%d %b"),
    `Zones (susp.)`  = sum_susp,
    `Nat. (susp.)`   = nat_susp,
    `Δ susp.`        = d_susp,
    `Zones (conf.)`  = sum_conf,
    `Nat. (conf.)`   = nat_conf,
    `Δ conf.`        = d_conf,
    `Zones (deaths)` = sum_dths,
    `Nat. (deaths)`  = nat_dths,
    `Δ deaths`       = d_dths
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "Suspected", "Confirmed", "Deaths"),
    colwidths = c(1, 3, 3, 3)
  ) |>
  merge_h(part = "header") |>
  align(align = "center", part = "header") |>
  align(j = -1, align = "right", part = "body") |>
  bg(i = discrepant_rows, bg = "#FFF3CD") |>
  colformat_int(j = 2:10, na_str = "—") |>
  bold(part = "header") |>
  hline(part = "body") |>
  autofit()
```

### Data quality notes — case counts

Two issues in the Mongbwalu data are worth noting. First, the early INRB-UMIE data shows 246 suspected cases at Mongbwalu on 14 May. Our earliest report (SitRep 001) records the same 246 figure, but only as a total for the whole of Ituri province — it does not break the number down by health zone. The first report with a Mongbwalu-specific count in our data is SitRep 004 (18 May, 302 suspected cases). As a result, the Mongbwalu line in the figures below starts later than in the Kraemer data; this is a gap in zone-level reporting, not a sign that cases only began in May.

Second, both our data and the INRB-UMIE data show a fall in cumulative confirmed cases at Mongbwalu between 21 May (14 cases) and 22 May (10 cases). Cumulative case counts should not decrease, so this is likely a downward revision in the 22 May situation report rather than an error in how the data were extracted. In our figures the value is held at 14 on 22 May (since we do not allow counts to fall); the INRB-UMIE series shows the raw figure.

At the right edge of the case-count comparison panels, the automated extraction now extends to 27 May (SitRep 013) while the latest zone-level values in the INRB-UMIE dataset are from 26 May. Any apparent divergence on the final date therefore reflects a one-day coverage gap rather than a methodological disagreement.

```{r}
#| label: fig-comparison
#| fig-cap: "Cross-source comparison of cumulative confirmed cases, suspect/probable cases, and outbreak deaths by health zone. Solid lines: automated extraction (raw); dashed lines: INRB-UMIE (manual extraction). Both automated and INRB-UMIE sources use identical PDFs; divergence reflects extraction method differences."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 14
PAL_COMP <- c(
  "Automated extraction" = "#0072B2",
  "INRB-UMIE"            = "#E69F00"
)
LTY_COMP <- c(
  "Automated extraction" = "solid",
  "INRB-UMIE"            = "dashed"
)
POINT_SIZE_OURS <- 2.2
POINT_SIZE_KRAEMER <- 1.7

zone_province_lut <- zone_ts |>
  distinct(zone, province) |>
  mutate(zone = as.character(zone))

our_for_comp <- zone_ts |>
  transmute(zone = as.character(zone), province, date,
            confirmed   = confirmed_raw,
            suspected   = unconfirmed_raw,
            deaths_susp = deaths_susp_raw,
            deaths      = deaths_raw,
            source      = "Automated extraction")

# National totals — ours (raw extracted values, no cummax enforcement)
our_nat_for_comp <- ous_national |>
  transmute(zone     = "National",
            province = "National",
            date,
            confirmed   = ours_confirmed,
            suspected   = ours_suspected,
            deaths_susp = ours_deaths,
            deaths      = ours_deaths,
            source      = "Automated extraction")

# INRB-UMIE deaths_susp = suspected only; deaths = suspected + confirmed (total)
kraemer_for_comp <- kraemer |>
  left_join(zone_province_lut, by = "zone") |>
  transmute(zone, province, date, confirmed, suspected,
            deaths_susp, deaths,
            source)

# INRB-UMIE national totals
kraemer_nat_for_comp <- parsed_kraemer_nat |>
  transmute(zone     = "National",
            province = "National",
            date,
            confirmed   = kraemer_confirmed,
            suspected   = kraemer_suspected,
            deaths_susp = kraemer_susp_deaths,
            deaths      = coalesce(kraemer_susp_deaths, 0) +
                          coalesce(kraemer_conf_deaths, 0),
            source      = "INRB-UMIE")

comp_long <- bind_rows(our_nat_for_comp, kraemer_nat_for_comp,
                       our_for_comp, kraemer_for_comp) |>
  pivot_longer(c(confirmed, suspected, deaths_susp, deaths),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "count") |>
  mutate(
    metric = recode(metric,
      "confirmed"   = "Confirmed cases",
      "suspected"   = "Suspect/probable cases",
      "deaths_susp" = "Suspected deaths",
      "deaths"      = "Total deaths (coalesced)"
    ),
    metric   = factor(metric, levels = c("Confirmed cases", "Suspect/probable cases",
                                          "Suspected deaths", "Total deaths (coalesced)")),
    source   = factor(source, levels = c("Automated extraction", "INRB-UMIE")),
    province = factor(province, levels = c("National", "Ituri", "Nord-Kivu")),
    zone     = factor(zone, levels = c("National", ZONE_LEVELS))
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(count))

comp_long_nat   <- filter(comp_long, zone == "National")
comp_long_zones <- filter(comp_long, zone != "National")

base_theme <- list(
  scale_colour_manual(values = PAL_COMP),
  scale_linetype_manual(values = LTY_COMP),
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%d %b", date_breaks = "2 days"),
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = int_breaks, limits = c(0, NA),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))),
  theme_sitrep(base_size = 9),
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "grey70", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.6, "lines")
  )
)

p_nat <- ggplot(comp_long_nat,
                aes(x = date, y = count, colour = source, linetype = source)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_point(data = ~filter(.x, source == "Automated extraction"),
             size = POINT_SIZE_OURS,    na.rm = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(data = ~filter(.x, source == "INRB-UMIE"),
             size = POINT_SIZE_KRAEMER, na.rm = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Cumulative count", colour = NULL, linetype = NULL,
       title = "National") +
  base_theme

p_zones <- ggplot(comp_long_zones,
                  aes(x = date, y = count, colour = source, linetype = source)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_point(data = ~filter(.x, source == "Automated extraction"),
             size = POINT_SIZE_OURS,    na.rm = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(data = ~filter(.x, source == "INRB-UMIE"),
             size = POINT_SIZE_KRAEMER, na.rm = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_nested(metric ~ province + zone, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, colour = NULL, linetype = NULL,
       title = "By health zone") +
  base_theme +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

p <- (p_nat | p_zones) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 5), guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(
    title    = "MVE DRC \u2014 Cross-source validation",
    subtitle = "Cumulative counts: automated extraction (raw) vs INRB-UMIE (manual extraction, same source PDFs)",
    caption  = "Sources: INSP DRC situation reports; github.com/INRB-UMIE/Ebola_DRC_2026",
    theme    = theme_sitrep(base_size = 9)
  ) &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
ggsave(here("outputs", "plots", "fig-comparison.png"), plot = p, width = 12, height = 14, dpi = 300)
knitr::include_graphics(here("outputs", "plots", "fig-comparison.png"))
```

Table 2 presents numerical differences at the most recent date available in each source for each zone.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-comparison
#| tbl-cap: "Table 2. Cumulative case counts at the latest available date per zone: automated extraction vs INRB-UMIE. Delta = ours minus INRB-UMIE. Dashes indicate no data in that source."
ours_latest <- zone_ts |>
  group_by(zone) |>
  slice_max(date, n = 1) |>
  ungroup() |>
  transmute(zone, conf_ours = confirmed, susp_ours = unconfirmed, dead_ours = deaths)

kraemer_latest <- kraemer |>
  group_by(zone) |>
  slice_max(date, n = 1) |>
  ungroup() |>
  transmute(zone, conf_them = confirmed, susp_them = suspected, dead_them = deaths)

full_join(ours_latest, kraemer_latest, by = "zone") |>
  mutate(
    d_conf = conf_ours - conf_them,
    d_susp = susp_ours - susp_them,
    d_dead = dead_ours - dead_them
  ) |>
  arrange(zone) |>
  transmute(
    zone,
    conf_ours, conf_them, d_conf,
    susp_ours, susp_them, d_susp,
    dead_ours, dead_them, d_dead
  ) |>
  setNames(c(
    "Health zone",
    "Confirmed (ours)", "Confirmed (INRB-UMIE)", "\u0394 confirmed",
    "Suspect/prob. (ours)", "Suspect/prob. (INRB-UMIE)", "\u0394 suspect/prob.",
    "Deaths (ours)", "Deaths (INRB-UMIE)", "\u0394 deaths"
  )) |>
  flextable() |>
  bold(part = "header") |>
  bg(part = "header", bg = "#1A3A5C") |>
  color(part = "header", color = "white") |>
  align(j = 2:10, align = "right", part = "all") |>
  colformat_int(j = 2:10, na_str = "\u2014") |>
  autofit()
```

### Response indicators

```{r}
#| label: load-response-ours
resp_ours <- read_csv(
  here::here("outputs", "master_response_counts.csv"),
  col_types      = cols(.default = "c"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) |>
  mutate(
    date   = dmy(date),
    zone   = factor(zone, levels = ZONE_LEVELS),
    across(c(in_bed_previous_day, new_admissions_ppl, new_admissions_other,
             total_admissions, total_isolated, cumulative_admitted),
           ~suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.x))),
    source = "Automated extraction"
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(zone), !zone %in% c("Total"))

poe_ours <- read_csv(
  here::here("outputs", "master_poe_counts.csv"),
  col_types      = cols(.default = "c"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) |>
  mutate(
    date = dmy(date),
    across(c(total_passed, total_screened, total_handwashing, total_sensitised),
           ~suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.x))),
    source = "Automated extraction"
  ) |>
  select(date, total_passed, total_screened, total_handwashing, total_sensitised, source)
```

### Data quality notes — response indicators

Two points are worth flagging when comparing the response indicator figures. For Rwampara on 21 May, SitRep 007 splits the data across three sub-columns without explaining what each represents. Both datasets handle this differently, so the 21 May Rwampara figures should be treated with caution. For 20–21 May more broadly, SitReps 005 and 006 used an earlier table layout that made the in-bed count and new admissions columns harder to distinguish; the two datasets differ slightly on those dates as a result. From 22 May onwards, when the table format was standardised, the datasets are in close agreement.

The INRB-UMIE processed sitrep files currently extend through 26 May for hospital indicators and through 26 May for PoE indicators (both updated to build `2026-05-28`), while our automated extraction now covers through 27 May (SitRep 013). The validation panels therefore overlap fully through 26 May; any gap at the right edge of those panels reflects a one-day coverage difference rather than a methodological discrepancy.

```{r}
#| label: fig-comparison-response
#| fig-cap: "Cross-source comparison of zone-level response indicators. Solid lines: automated extraction; dashed lines: INRB-UMIE. Both series treat the ambiguous 21 May Rwampara sub-columns differently; values for that date should be interpreted cautiously. The two datasets differ slightly on 20–21 May due to an ambiguous table layout in SitReps 005–006, and are in close agreement from 22 May onwards."
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 9
PAL_RESP <- c("Automated extraction" = "#0072B2", "INRB-UMIE" = "#E69F00")
LTY_RESP <- c("Automated extraction" = "solid",   "INRB-UMIE" = "dashed")

resp_comp <- bind_rows(
  resp_ours |>
    transmute(date, zone,
              hospitalised        = total_isolated,
              in_bed_previous_day,
              total_admissions,
              source),
  kraemer_response |>
    transmute(date, zone = factor(zone, levels = ZONE_LEVELS),
              hospitalised,
              in_bed_previous_day,
              total_admissions,
              source)
) |>
  pivot_longer(c(hospitalised, in_bed_previous_day, total_admissions),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "count") |>
  mutate(
    metric = recode(metric,
      "hospitalised"        = "Currently hospitalised",
      "in_bed_previous_day" = "In bed (prev. day)",
      "total_admissions"    = "New admissions (total)"
    ),
    metric = factor(metric, levels = c("Currently hospitalised",
                                        "In bed (prev. day)",
                                        "New admissions (total)")),
    source = factor(source, levels = c("Automated extraction", "INRB-UMIE"))
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(count), !is.na(zone))

p <- ggplot(resp_comp, aes(x = date, y = count, colour = source, linetype = source)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_point(data = ~filter(.x, source == "Automated extraction"), size = POINT_SIZE_OURS, na.rm = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(data = ~filter(.x, source == "INRB-UMIE"), size = POINT_SIZE_KRAEMER, na.rm = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_nested(metric ~ zone, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = PAL_RESP) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = LTY_RESP) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%d %b", date_breaks = "2 days") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = int_breaks, limits = c(0, NA),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title    = "MVE DRC \u2014 Response indicators — cross-source validation",
    subtitle = "Automated extraction vs INRB-UMIE (manual, same source PDFs)",
    x = NULL, y = "Count", colour = NULL, linetype = NULL,
    caption  = "Sources: INSP DRC situation reports; github.com/INRB-UMIE/Ebola_DRC_2026"
  ) +
  theme_sitrep(base_size = 9) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "grey70", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "lines")
  )
ggsave(here("outputs", "plots", "fig-comparison-response.png"), plot = p,
       width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)
knitr::include_graphics(here("outputs", "plots", "fig-comparison-response.png"))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-comparison-poe
#| fig-cap: "Cross-source comparison of daily Point of Entry (POE) screening totals. Automated extraction reports a single aggregate per sitrep date; INRB-UMIE data are summed across individual checkpoints to produce a daily total."
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
poe_comp <- bind_rows(poe_ours, kraemer_poe) |>
  pivot_longer(c(total_passed, total_screened, total_handwashing, total_sensitised),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "count") |>
  mutate(
    metric = recode(metric,
      "total_passed"      = "Total passed",
      "total_screened"    = "Screened",
      "total_handwashing" = "Handwashing stations",
      "total_sensitised"  = "Sensitised"
    ),
    metric = factor(metric, levels = c("Total passed", "Screened",
                                        "Handwashing stations", "Sensitised")),
    source = factor(source, levels = c("Automated extraction", "INRB-UMIE"))
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(count))

p <- ggplot(poe_comp, aes(x = date, y = count, colour = source, linetype = source)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_point(data = ~filter(.x, source == "Automated extraction"), size = POINT_SIZE_OURS + 0.3, na.rm = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(data = ~filter(.x, source == "INRB-UMIE"), size = POINT_SIZE_KRAEMER + 0.3, na.rm = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = PAL_RESP) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = LTY_RESP) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%d %b", date_breaks = "2 days") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = int_breaks, limits = c(0, NA),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))) +
  labs(
    title    = "MVE DRC \u2014 POE screening — cross-source validation",
    subtitle = "Daily aggregate totals: automated extraction vs INRB-UMIE (sum across checkpoints; INRB-UMIE extends to 26 May)",
    x = NULL, y = "Count", colour = NULL, linetype = NULL,
    caption  = "Sources: INSP DRC situation reports; github.com/INRB-UMIE/Ebola_DRC_2026"
  ) +
  theme_sitrep(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "grey70", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.6, "lines")
  )
ggsave(here("outputs", "plots", "fig-comparison-poe.png"), plot = p,
       width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300)
knitr::include_graphics(here("outputs", "plots", "fig-comparison-poe.png"))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-comparison-contacts
#| fig-cap: "Cross-source comparison of cumulative contacts traced by health zone. Solid lines: automated extraction (raw); dashed lines: INRB-UMIE (manual extraction). ND values excluded."
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7
contacts_ours <- df |>
  filter(
    count_type == "Cumules",
    zone %in% ZONE_LEVELS,
    !is.na(contacts), contacts != ""
  ) |>
  mutate(contacts = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(contacts)),
         zone = factor(zone, levels = ZONE_LEVELS)) |>
  filter(!is.na(contacts)) |>
  arrange(zone, date) |>
  group_by(zone) |>
  ungroup() |>
  transmute(date, zone, contacts, source = "Automated extraction")

contacts_kraemer <- kraemer_contacts |>
  filter(zone %in% ZONE_LEVELS) |>
  transmute(date, zone = factor(zone, levels = ZONE_LEVELS),
            contacts = contacts_traced, source = "INRB-UMIE")

contacts_comp <- bind_rows(contacts_ours, contacts_kraemer) |>
  filter(!is.na(contacts)) |>
  left_join(zone_province_lut, by = "zone") |>
  mutate(
    source   = factor(source, levels = c("Automated extraction", "INRB-UMIE")),
    province = factor(province, levels = c("Ituri", "Nord-Kivu")),
    zone     = factor(zone, levels = ZONE_LEVELS)
  )

p <- ggplot(contacts_comp,
            aes(x = date, y = contacts, colour = source, linetype = source)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_point(data = ~filter(.x, source == "Automated extraction"), size = POINT_SIZE_OURS, na.rm = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(data = ~filter(.x, source == "INRB-UMIE"), size = POINT_SIZE_KRAEMER, na.rm = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_nested(~ province + zone, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = PAL_COMP[c("Automated extraction","INRB-UMIE")]) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = LTY_COMP[c("Automated extraction","INRB-UMIE")]) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%d %b", date_breaks = "2 days") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = int_breaks, limits = c(0, NA),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title    = "MVE DRC \u2014 Cumulative contacts traced — cross-source validation",
    subtitle = "Automated extraction (raw) vs INRB-UMIE (manual extraction, same source PDFs)",
    x = NULL, y = "Cumulative contacts traced", colour = NULL, linetype = NULL,
    caption  = "Sources: INSP DRC situation reports; github.com/INRB-UMIE/Ebola_DRC_2026"
  ) +
  theme_sitrep(base_size = 9) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "grey70", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "lines")
  )
ggsave(here("outputs", "plots", "fig-comparison-contacts.png"), plot = p,
       width = 11, height = 7, dpi = 300)
knitr::include_graphics(here("outputs", "plots", "fig-comparison-contacts.png"))
```

## Discussion

Case counts have risen steadily over the reporting period. Confirmed cases remain a small fraction of all reported cases, which is typical in field settings where laboratory testing capacity is limited. The outbreak spans health zones in both Ituri and Nord-Kivu, pointing to the need for a coordinated response across provincial boundaries.

A few limitations are worth keeping in mind. Earlier situation reports did not break case counts down by health zone, so the zone-level data only covers part of the outbreak period. Response indicators such as hospitalisations and contacts traced are not formatted consistently across reports, which makes trend comparisons harder. Comparing our automated extraction with the INRB-UMIE manual series provides a useful cross-check: where the two agree we can be more confident in the figures; where they differ, it usually points to genuine ambiguity in the source document that is worth flagging to the data providers.
